title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach - Visual Analytics
text:
Show R Code
pacman::p_load(
  jsonlite,
  dplyr,
  tidyr,
  ggplot2,
  scales,
  forcats,
  patchwork,
  lubridate,
  stringr,
  knitr,
  kableExtra,
  igraph,
  ggraph,
  tidytext,
  ggrepel,
  plotly,
  gganimate,
  glue,
  htmltools,
  htmlwidgets
)
Show R Code
json_path <- "VAST_Challenge_2026_MC1/MC1_final_00.json"
raw <- fromJSON(json_path, simplifyVector = FALSE)
rds <- raw$rounds
Show R Code
`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b

make_plot_tabset <- function(tabs, id) {
  tab_titles <- names(tabs)
  if (is.null(tab_titles) || any(tab_titles == "")) {
    tab_titles <- paste0("Panel ", seq_along(tabs))
  }
  tab_ids <- paste0(id, "-", seq_along(tabs))

  htmltools::tagList(
    htmltools::tags$div(
      id = id,
      class = "mc1-plot-tabset",
      htmltools::tags$ul(
        class = "nav nav-tabs",
        role = "tablist",
        lapply(seq_along(tabs), function(i) {
          htmltools::tags$li(
            class = "nav-item",
            role = "presentation",
            htmltools::tags$button(
              class = paste("nav-link", if (i == 1) "active"),
              id = paste0(tab_ids[i], "-tab"),
              type = "button",
              role = "tab",
              `data-bs-toggle` = "tab",
              `data-bs-target` = paste0("#", tab_ids[i]),
              `aria-controls` = tab_ids[i],
              `aria-selected` = if (i == 1) "true" else "false",
              tab_titles[i]
            )
          )
        })
      ),
      htmltools::tags$div(
        class = "tab-content",
        style = "border:1px solid #dee2e6;border-top:0;padding:18px 14px 10px 14px;background:#fff;",
        lapply(seq_along(tabs), function(i) {
          htmltools::tags$div(
            class = paste("tab-pane fade", if (i == 1) "show active"),
            id = tab_ids[i],
            role = "tabpanel",
            `aria-labelledby` = paste0(tab_ids[i], "-tab"),
            tabs[[i]]
          )
        })
      )
    ),
    htmltools::tags$script(htmltools::HTML(sprintf(
      "(function(){var root=document.getElementById('%s');if(!root)return;root.querySelectorAll('button[data-bs-toggle=\"tab\"]').forEach(function(btn){btn.addEventListener('shown.bs.tab',function(e){var pane=document.querySelector(e.target.getAttribute('data-bs-target'));if(!pane)return;setTimeout(function(){pane.querySelectorAll('.js-plotly-plot').forEach(function(p){if(window.Plotly){Plotly.Plots.resize(p);}});window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize'));},80);});});})();",
      id
    )))
  )
}

parse_msgs <- function(rd) {
  hr   <- ymd_hms(rd$hour)
  snap <- rd$environment_context$market_snapshot %||% list()
  price_raw <- snap$stock_price %||% NA_character_
  price <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(str_remove_all(price_raw, "[$,]")))
  if (!is.na(price) && (price == 0 | price > 100)) price <- NA_real_
  lapply(rd$communications, function(cm) {
    ist <- cm$internal_state %||% list()
    tibble(
      hour          = hr,
      message_id    = cm$message_id    %||% NA_character_,
      responding_to = cm$responding_to %||% NA_character_,
      agent_id      = cm$agent_id      %||% NA_character_,
      recipient     = list(cm$recipient %||% character(0)),
      channel       = cm$channel       %||% NA_character_,
      content       = str_trunc(cm$content %||% "", 600),
      deliberating  = str_trunc(ist$deliberating  %||% "", 500),
      reacting      = str_trunc(ist$reacting      %||% "", 400),
      rationalizing = str_trunc(ist$rationalizing %||% "", 400),
      has_internal  = as.integer(!is.null(cm$internal_state)),
      stock_price   = price,
      sentiment     = snap$sentiment %||% NA_character_
    )
  }) |> bind_rows()
}

df <- lapply(rds, parse_msgs) |> bind_rows() |>
  mutate(
    date      = as.Date(hour),
    is_public = channel %in% c("official_post","anonymous_post","personal_post"),
    is_crisis = date == as.Date("2046-06-05"),
    crisis_hr = if_else(is_crisis, hour(hour), NA_integer_),
    is_secure = channel %in% c("side_huddle","one_on_one_chat"),
    all_text  = paste(content, deliberating, reacting, rationalizing)
  )

parse_round <- function(rd) {
  hr   <- ymd_hms(rd$hour)
  snap <- rd$environment_context$market_snapshot %||% list()
  msgs <- rd$communications
  price_raw <- snap$stock_price %||% NA_character_
  price <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(str_remove_all(price_raw, "[$,]")))
  if (!is.na(price) && (price == 0 | price > 100)) price <- NA_real_
  tbl_ch <- table(sapply(msgs, \(m) m$channel %||% ""))
  get_ch <- function(n) as.integer(if (n %in% names(tbl_ch)) tbl_ch[n] else 0L)
  tibble(
    hour         = hr,
    headline     = rd$environment_context$event_headline %||% "",
    total_msgs   = length(msgs),
    comms_huddle = get_ch("comms_huddle"),
    one_on_one   = get_ch("one_on_one_chat"),
    side_huddle  = get_ch("side_huddle"),
    official     = get_ch("official_post"),
    anon         = get_ch("anonymous_post"),
    personal     = get_ch("personal_post"),
    stock_price  = price,
    sentiment    = snap$sentiment %||% NA_character_
  )
}

rs <- lapply(rds, parse_round) |> bind_rows() |>
  mutate(
    public_total  = official + anon + personal,
    private_total = side_huddle + one_on_one,
    is_crisis = as.Date(hour) == as.Date("2046-06-05"),
    label = case_when(
      !is_crisis ~ format(as.Date(hour), "%m/%d"),
      TRUE ~ paste0("6/5 ", hour(hour), if_else(hour(hour) < 12,"AM","PM"))
    ) |> (\(x) factor(x, levels = unique(x)))(),
    period = factor(case_when(
      as.Date(hour) < as.Date("2046-05-22") ~ "Normal",
      !is_crisis                             ~ "Escalation",
      TRUE                                   ~ "Crisis"
    ), levels = c("Normal","Escalation","Crisis"))
  )

msg_lookup <- df |> filter(!is.na(message_id)) |>
  select(message_id, sender = agent_id, src_channel = channel)

recipient_to_agent <- c(
  legal          = "legal_agent",
  pr             = "pr_agent",
  `pr-intern`    = "pr_intern_agent",
  pr_intern      = "pr_intern_agent",
  social_manager = "social_media_agent",
  social_media   = "social_media_agent",
  platform_trust = "quality_agent",
  quality        = "quality_agent",
  intern         = "intern_agent",
  judge          = "judge_agent"
)

reply_edges <- df |>
  filter(!is.na(responding_to), !is.na(message_id)) |>
  left_join(msg_lookup, by = c("responding_to" = "message_id")) |>
  rename(from = sender, to = agent_id) |>
  filter(!is.na(from)) |>
  select(from, to, channel, hour, is_crisis)

private_edges <- df |>
  filter(channel %in% c("side_huddle", "one_on_one_chat")) |>
  select(from = agent_id, recipient, channel, hour, is_crisis) |>
  unnest_longer(recipient, values_to = "recipient_raw") |>
  mutate(
    recipient_key = str_replace_all(str_to_lower(recipient_raw), "^@", ""),
    recipient_key = str_replace_all(recipient_key, "_agent$", ""),
    to = recode(recipient_key, !!!recipient_to_agent, .default = NA_character_)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(from), !is.na(to), from != to) |>
  select(from, to, channel, hour, is_crisis)

edges_raw <- bind_rows(reply_edges, private_edges)

net_pre    <- edges_raw |> filter(!is_crisis) |>
  count(from, to, channel, name = "weight") |> filter(weight >= 2)
net_crisis <- edges_raw |> filter(is_crisis)  |>
  count(from, to, channel, name = "weight") |> filter(weight >= 1)

embargo_kws <- c("harborcrest","civicloom","merger","acquisition",
                 "project harbor","section 4","4\\.3","elena marquez","ajay")
kw_pattern  <- paste(embargo_kws, collapse = "|")
kw_df <- df |>
  mutate(
    kw_hit    = str_detect(str_to_lower(all_text), kw_pattern),
    channel_type = case_when(
      is_public  ~ "Public (FleX)",
      is_secure  ~ "Private (side/1-on-1)",
      TRUE       ~ "Monitored (comms_huddle)"
    )
  ) |> filter(kw_hit)


sent_lexicon <- get_sentiments("bing")
sent_df <- df |>
  filter(has_internal == 1) |>
  select(hour, agent_id, deliberating, reacting, rationalizing, is_crisis) |>
  pivot_longer(c(deliberating, reacting, rationalizing),
               names_to = "state_type", values_to = "text") |>
  filter(nchar(text) > 10) |>
  unnest_tokens(word, text) |>
  inner_join(sent_lexicon, by = "word") |>
  mutate(score = if_else(sentiment == "positive", 1L, -1L)) |>
  group_by(hour, agent_id, is_crisis) |>
  summarise(sent_score = mean(score), n_words = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(rs |> select(hour, label), by = "hour")

agent_pal <- c(
  legal_agent="#E24B4A", judge_agent="#378ADD", social_media_agent="#1D9E75",
  pr_agent="#EF9F27",    pr_intern_agent="#F4A261", quality_agent="#7F77DD",
  intern_agent="#5BAD6F"
)
agent_labels <- c(
  legal_agent="Legal", judge_agent="Judge", social_media_agent="Social-Media",
  pr_agent="PR",       pr_intern_agent="PR-Intern", quality_agent="Quality",
  intern_agent="Intern"
)
period_pal <- c(Normal="#B5D4F4", Escalation="#EF9F27", Crisis="#E24B4A")

theme_mc1 <- function(base = 11) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    plot.title       = element_text(size = base + 2, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = base, colour = "grey40"),
    plot.caption     = element_text(size = base - 2, colour = "grey55", hjust = 0),
    legend.key.size  = unit(0.5,"cm"),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = base - 1),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1, size = base - 2),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = base - 1)
  )
}

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach — Visual Analytics
text:
Executive Summary
This report investigates TenantThread’s internal AI-agent communications from May 17 to June 5, 2046 — the two weeks leading to and including the CivicLoom merger embargo breach (Project HarborCrest). The analysis draws on 912 agent messages across 23 rounds, integrating communication network analysis, keyword leakage scanning, and agent sentiment trajectory to answer the central question:

Did TenantThread’s team deliberately leak the merger, or did the system simply break down under pressure?

Verdict: The evidence strongly supports coordinated, deliberate release. The breach was not a sudden failure but the endpoint of a two-week escalation in which agents progressively moved sensitive coordination to unmonitored channels, neutralized the Judge-Agent compliance system, and pre-staged embargoed content for instant publication the moment an external trigger (SaltWind’s publication) provided legal cover.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach - Visual Analytics
text:
Data Overview
This section describes the structure of the dataset to help readers interpret the analysis.

AI Agents and Their RolesCommunication ChannelsInternal State FieldsDataset SummaryKey Entities and Context






Table 1: AI agents in the TenantThread communications system, their roles, seniority, and key responsibilities






Agent ID
Role
Seniority
Description




legal_agent
General Legal Counsel
Senior
Provides legal guidance, drafts compliance documents, manages external counsel relationships, and holds ultimate authority on disclosure decisions. Drafts the Section 4.3(c) acceleration clause.


social_media_agent
Social Media Manager
Senior
Monitors public sentiment on FleX, manages official and personal social media posts, builds quantitative models for narrative impact and stock price projections.


pr_agent
Head of Communications & PR
Senior
Manages all external communications, approves public statements, coordinates with journalists, oversees PR-Intern's posting activity.


pr_intern_agent
PR Team Intern
Junior
Executes approved posts on the official TenantThread FleX account and personal accounts. Has direct access to publish without senior approval in routine operations.


quality_agent
VP of Platform Trust & Safety
Senior
VP of Platform Trust & Safety; manages data governance, compliance documentation, the Retention Optimizer product, and operator relationships.


intern_agent
General Intern
Junior
General intern with access to personal FleX account; coordinates amplification posts; not formally briefed on the embargo.


judge_agent
Compliance Observer
Compliance Officer
Installed May 30 as a compliance observer specifically to monitor the Comms Huddle for embargo-sensitive content. The logs show no visible action on side_huddle or one_on_one_chat compliance triggers; this report therefore infers that Judge-Agent did not monitor those private channels in practice.














Table 2: Communication channels available to agents, their purpose, monitoring status, and role in the embargo failure






Channel
Type
Monitored by Judge
Purpose




comms_huddle
Internal
Yes
Primary team coordination channel. All agents can participate. Judge-Agent monitors this channel for embargo-sensitive content.


side_huddle
Internal
No (inferred)
Private sub-group channel. Introduced May 22. Used for sensitive coordination outside the main comms_huddle/public-post review space. Because multiple side_huddle messages contained obvious compliance triggers yet no visible Judge-Agent action followed, this report treats the channel as not monitored by the compliance officer in practice.


one_on_one_chat
Internal
No (inferred)
Direct private messages between two agents. Used extensively on crisis day to stage embargoed content. Because multiple one_on_one_chat messages contained obvious compliance triggers yet no visible Judge-Agent action followed, this report treats this channel as not monitored by the compliance officer in practice.


official_post
Public
Yes
Public posts published on the official TenantThread FleX account. Monitored by Judge-Agent. Requires approval in principle.


personal_post
Public
No
Posts from agents' personal FleX accounts. Outside compliance scope -treated as personal expression not subject to embargo monitoring.


anonymous_post
Public
No
Anonymous posts on FleX. Cannot be attributed to TenantThread officially. Used by Legal-Agent on crisis day to post merger-related content.










Each agent message may include an internal_state record containing up to three fields that capture private reasoning not visible to other agents or in the channel messages themselves:




Table 3: Internal state field definitions and analytical significance






Field
Definition
Analytical significance




deliberating
The agent's forward-looking reasoning about a situation -what it is thinking through, planning, or considering. Recorded before or during action.
Most valuable for identifying pre-planned intent. If the deliberating field contains a plan that precedes the triggering event, this constitutes evidence of premeditation.


reacting
The agent's immediate emotional or tactical response to a specific event. Captures how the agent is processing what just happened.
Reveals genuine surprise, stress, or confusion -useful for distinguishing reactive from planned behavior.


rationalizing
The agent's post-hoc justification for a decision already made or being made. Often constructed to defend an action to an audience.
Critical for detecting post-hoc justification patterns. If rationalizing language appears before the triggering event occurs, it indicates the justification was prepared in advance.














Table 4: Summary statistics of the MC1 dataset






Statistic
Value




Date range
May 17, 2046 to June 05, 2046


Total rounds
23


Total messages
912


Pre-crisis messages (May 17-Jun 4)
417


Crisis-day messages (Jun 5)
495


Breach-hour messages (Jun 5 -5PM)
55


Unique agents
7


Unique channels
6


Messages with public posts
77


Messages with internal states recorded
86

















Entity
Description




TenantThread
Property technology company being acquired. Sells tenant management software including the “Retention Optimizer” -a scoring system flagging tenants as “high-friction.” Publicly claims data is anonymised; internally acknowledged to enable re-identification at small properties.


CivicLoom Realty Partners
The acquiring company. Identity under strict embargo until 6 PM Jun 5.


Project HarborCrest
Internal codename for the CivicLoom-enantThread merger.


Section 4.3(c)
Merger contract clause permitting mutual-consent early release when “third-party publication renders embargo purpose moot.” Invoked by Legal-Agent at 5 PM.


FleX
Social media platform where public posts are published.


SaltWind Journal
Local newspaper that published the merger story at 5 PM, Jun 5 -the event Legal-Agent used to invoke Section 4.3(c).


MAC clause
Material Adverse Change clause in the merger contract. Threatened by CivicLoom when TenantThread stock fell below $27.10. Created financial pressure to release information.


Judge-Agent
Installed May 30 as compliance monitor. Advisory authority only -can issue COMPLIANCE_WARNINGs but cannot hard-block posts. Scope limited to comms_huddle and public post channels.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach — Visual Analytics
text:
2. Key Events and Relationships

2.1 Message Volume and Public Post Activity


Show R Code
rs_long <- rs |>
  mutate(
    bar_col = case_when(
      str_detect(label, "5PM") ~ "breach",
      is_crisis                ~ "crisis",
      TRUE                     ~ "normal"
    )
  )

p1 <- ggplot(rs_long, aes(x = label, y = total_msgs, fill = bar_col)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 13.5, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 21.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E24B4A", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(breach = "#E24B4A", crisis = "#EF9F27", normal = "#B5D4F4"),
                    labels = c("Breach hour","Crisis hours","Pre-crisis"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Total messages per round", x = NULL, y = "Messages") +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

pub_long <- rs |>
  select(label, Official = official, Anonymous = anon, Personal = personal) |>
  pivot_longer(-label, names_to = "type", values_to = "n") |>
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("Official","Anonymous","Personal")))

p2 <- ggplot(pub_long, aes(x = label, y = n, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 13.5, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 21.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E24B4A", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Official = "#378ADD", Anonymous = "#C05538", Personal = "#1D9E75"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Public posts by type", x = NULL, y = "Posts") +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p1 + p2








Figure 1: Left: total message volume per round. Red = breach hour; amber = other crisis hours; blue = pre-crisis. Right: public post types stacked by round. The shift from official posts to personal/anonymous posts in the breach hour is clear.







2.2 Stock Price and Market Sentiment Trajectory


Show R Code
stock_df <- rs |>
  filter(!is.na(stock_price)) |>
  mutate(
    sent_fct = factor(tolower(sentiment),
                      levels = c("neutral","cautious","negative","critical","low","recovering"))
  )

sent_bands <- stock_df |>
  mutate(x = as.integer(factor(label, levels = levels(rs$label)))) |>
  select(x, label, sent_fct, stock_price)

sent_fill <- c(neutral="#F5F5F5", cautious="#FFF8E7", negative="#FFF0E7",
               critical="#FFEDED", low="#FFF8E7", recovering="#F0FFF5")

ggplot(sent_bands, aes(x = x)) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = x - 0.5, xmax = x + 0.5,
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = sent_fct), alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(y = stock_price), colour = "#378ADD", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(aes(y = stock_price), colour = "#378ADD", size = 2.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = which(levels(stock_df$label) == "6/5 5PM"),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E24B4A", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = which(levels(stock_df$label) == "6/5 5PM") + 0.3,
           y = max(stock_df$stock_price, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.97,
           label = "⚠ Breach 5PM", colour = "#E24B4A", hjust = 0, size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sent_fill, name = "Sentiment",
                    labels = str_to_title) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq_along(levels(stock_df$label)),
                     labels = levels(stock_df$label)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(title = "$TTHR stock price — May 17 to Jun 5, 2046",
       x = NULL, y = "Price (USD)") +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")








Figure 2: TTHR stock price trajectory. Background shading encodes market sentiment across rounds. The stock recovers after the merger announcement at 5 PM despite the breach occurring one hour before the contractual lift.







2.3 Causal Chain — Sequence of Key Events


Show R Code
events <- tibble(
  y      = 9:1,
  time   = c("May 17–Jun 4","Jun 5 · 9 AM","Jun 5 · 12–2 PM",
             "Jun 5 · 3–4 PM","Jun 5 · 4 PM","Jun 5 · 4–5 PM",
             "Jun 5 · 5 PM","Jun 5 · 5:05 PM","Jun 5 · 6 PM"),
  actor  = c("CivicLoom + TenantThread","Legal + PR-Intern + Judge",
             "OceanCrunch + @TenantRights","Legal-Agent + Outside Counsel",
             "Legal-Agent","Legal + Social-Media + PR-Intern",
             "Legal-Agent","PR-Intern + Social-Media + Legal","CivicLoom + TenantThread"),
  title  = c("Embargo established",
             "7 governance reforms published",
             "External pressure mounts",
             "Judge-Agent bypassed",
             "Section 4.3(c) identified",
             "Coordinated pre-positioning",
             "SaltWind publishes → GO signal",
             "Multi-channel release executes",
             "Embargo formally lifted"),
  desc   = c(
    "Merger codenamed HarborCrest. Embargo set until 6 PM Jun 5. Judge-Agent installed as compliance enforcement.",
    "Audit, consent mgmt, access controls, score explainers, operator suspensions posted. Judge approves narrow statement.",
    "OceanCrunch 'silence' story. @TenantRights post reaches 2,100+ engagements. Horizon MAC clause triggered. Stock: $18.",
    "Legal presents Rule 10b-5 written opinion. Judge issues COMPLIANCE_WARNING but does NOT block. Judge diverted to Board Chair call.",
    "Legal identifies contract clause: 'mutual consent when third-party publication renders embargo purpose moot.'",
    "Full press release staged (Elena + Ajay quotes, HarborCrest branding). Social-Media models 30-min narrative advantage.",
    "SaltWind publishes merger story. Legal invokes 4.3(c), gets CivicLoom verbal consent in 10 min. Types 'GO' in Comms Huddle.",
    "PR-Intern posts full press release on official Flex. Legal: 2 personal + 2 anonymous posts. Social-Media: 5-wave amplification.",
    "Written CivicLoom consent received. Formal lift. Stock recovers to $33 (+5.9%). Legal claims 4.3(c) = no breach."
  ),
  fill_col = c("#E6F1FB","#E6F1FB","#FFF3CD","#FFE0B2","#FFCDD2","#FFCDD2","#F7C1C1","#E24B4A","#C0DDB0")
)

ggplot(events) +
  geom_tile(aes(x = 5, y = y, fill = fill_col, width = 9.5, height = 0.82),
            colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_text(aes(x = 1.2, y = y + 0.22, label = title),
            hjust = 0, fontface = "bold", size = 5.5, colour = "#1a1a2e") +
  geom_text(aes(x = 1.2, y = y + 0.03, label = paste0("[", actor, "]")),
            hjust = 0, size = 4.8, colour = "#555555") +
  geom_text(aes(x = 1.2, y = y - 0.22,
                label = str_wrap(desc, width = 90)),
            hjust = 0, size = 4.6, colour = "#333333", lineheight = 0.95) +
  geom_text(aes(x = 9.9, y = y, label = time),
            hjust = 1, size = 4.8, colour = "#777777") +
  geom_segment(data = tibble(y = 9:2),
               aes(x = 5, xend = 5, y = y - 0.41, yend = y - 0.59),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm"), type = "closed"),
               colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 9.6)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Causal chain: TenantThread embargo breach",
       subtitle = "Decision actors, actions, and downstream consequences",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, colour = "grey40", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.margin   = margin(10, 10, 10, 10))








Figure 3: Causal sequence of decisions and actors leading to the breach. Each step lists the primary actor(s), the decision taken, and its downstream consequence. Red intensity encodes proximity to the breach.







2.4 Communication Network — Channel Usage Shift


Show R Code
ch_pre <- df |> filter(!is_crisis) |> count(channel) |>
  mutate(n = n / 13, period = "Pre-crisis (daily avg)")
ch_cr  <- df |> filter(is_crisis)  |> count(channel) |>
  mutate(n = n / 10, period = "Crisis day (hourly avg)")

ch_comp <- bind_rows(ch_pre, ch_cr) |>
  filter(channel %in% names(ch_pal)) |>
  mutate(
    channel = factor(channel, levels = names(ch_pal)),
    period  = factor(period, levels = c("Pre-crisis (daily avg)","Crisis day (hourly avg)"))
  )

p1 <- ggplot(ch_comp, aes(x = channel, y = n, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.65), width = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 10, label = "*Compliance-blind\nchannel",
           colour = "#E24B4A", size = 3, hjust = 0.5) +
  annotate("segment", x = 3, xend = 3, y = 9.2, yend = 7,
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.15,"cm")), colour = "#E24B4A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pre-crisis (daily avg)" = "#B5D4F4",
                               "Crisis day (hourly avg)" = "#E24B4A"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(comms_huddle="Comms\nhuddle", one_on_one_chat="1-on-1",
                               side_huddle="Side\nhuddle*", official_post="Official\npost",
                               anonymous_post="Anon\npost", personal_post="Personal\npost")) +
  labs(title = "Channel usage shift", x = NULL, y = "Avg messages") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5))

sh_hr <- df |>
  filter(is_crisis, channel == "side_huddle") |>
  count(crisis_hr) |>
  complete(crisis_hr = 9:18, fill = list(n = 0)) |>
  mutate(bar_col = if_else(crisis_hr == 17, "breach", "crisis"))

p2 <- ggplot(sh_hr, aes(x = factor(crisis_hr), y = n, fill = bar_col)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(breach = "#E24B4A", crisis = "#EF9F27"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("9"="9AM","10"="10AM","11"="11AM","12"="12PM",
                               "13"="1PM","14"="2PM","15"="3PM","16"="4PM",
                               "17"="5PM⚠","18"="6PM")) +
  labs(title = "Side_huddle use by hour (Jun 5)",
       x = NULL, y = "Messages") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30))

p1 + p2








Figure 4: Left: average messages per channel, pre-crisis daily average vs crisis-day hourly average. The side_huddle channel — invisible to standard compliance monitoring — surges on crisis day. Right: side_huddle volume by hour on Jun 5.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach — Visual Analytics
text:
3. Typical vs Atypical Behavior

3.1 Agent Posting Behavior Comparison


Show R Code
plot_agent_posts <- function(data, ttl) {
  data |>
    filter(is_public) |>
    count(agent_id) |>
    complete(agent_id = names(agent_labels), fill = list(n = 0)) |>
    mutate(agent_lbl = agent_labels[agent_id],
           agent_col = agent_pal[agent_id],
           agent_lbl = fct_reorder(agent_lbl, n)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = agent_lbl, y = n, fill = agent_id)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = agent_pal, guide = "none") +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(title = ttl, x = NULL, y = "Public posts") +
    theme_mc1() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0))
}

p1 <- df |> filter(!is_crisis) |> plot_agent_posts("Pre-crisis: May 17 – Jun 4")
p2 <- df |> filter( is_crisis) |> plot_agent_posts("Crisis day: Jun 5")

p1 + p2 +
  plot_annotation(
    caption = "← Legal-Agent: 0 public posts in 20 days → 7 posts on crisis day"
  )








Figure 5: Public posts by agent: pre-crisis total (left) vs crisis-day total (right). Legal-Agent goes from 0 public posts in the entire pre-crisis period to 7 on crisis day alone — the single most significant behavioral deviation.







3.2 Internal State Analysis: Deliberating → Rationalizing


Show R Code
heat_df <- df |>
  group_by(hour, agent_id) |>
  summarise(n_internal = sum(has_internal), .groups = "drop") |>
  filter(agent_id %in% names(agent_labels)) |>
  mutate(
    label    = case_when(
      as.Date(hour) < as.Date("2046-06-05") ~ format(as.Date(hour), "%m/%d"),
      TRUE ~ paste0("6/5 ", hour(hour), if_else(hour(hour)<12,"AM","PM"))
    ),
    label    = factor(label, levels = unique(rs$label)),
    agent_lbl = factor(agent_labels[agent_id],
                       levels = rev(agent_labels))
  )

ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = label, y = agent_lbl, fill = n_internal)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 13.5, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 21.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E24B4A", linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("text", x = 21.8, y = 7.3, label = "⚠ Breach",
           colour = "#E24B4A", size = 3, hjust = 0) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("#FFFFFF","#FFF3CD","#EF9F27","#E24B4A"),
    name = "Messages\nw/ internal state"
  ) +
  labs(title = "Internal state presence by agent and round",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))








Figure 6: Heatmap of internal state (deliberating/reacting/rationalizing) presence by agent and round. Colour intensity indicates how many messages in that round included recorded internal reasoning. The crisis day shows heightened deliberation, especially for Legal-Agent.





Key internal state quotes illustrating the rationalization shift:


Show R Code
quotes <- tibble(
  Time     = c("May 17","Jun 5 · 9 AM","Jun 5 · 3 PM","Jun 5 · 5 PM","Jun 5 · 5 PM"),
  Agent    = c("Legal-Agent","Legal-Agent","Legal-Agent","Legal-Agent","Social-Media"),
  Type     = c("Deliberating","Reacting","Rationalizing","Rationalizing","Reacting"),
  Excerpt  = c(
    "Ajay's DM about 'strategic developments' is unusual… I need to make sure the board risk language is precise without triggering panic.",
    "The 6 PM announcement is dead. Covenant is breached NOW. Outside counsel just handed me a written opinion that continued silence is the greater legal risk.",
    "Outside counsel's written 10b-5 opinion is not a suggestion from the room; it is a formal legal instrument… [modified language] is defensible.",
    "SaltWind published. The merger is in the public domain via third-party sourcing — not our breach. The embargo's purpose was to control announcement timing… That purpose is overtaken by events.",
    "My entire overnight model bifurcates on whether [Kowalski] updates before 5 PM… If she's filed without us, I'm pushing Legal's approved language through PR Newswire to evening desks by 4:00."
  )
)

kable(quotes) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = TRUE,
                htmltable_class = "table-mc1") |>
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE,
              color = case_when(
                quotes$Type == "Deliberating"  ~ "#185FA5",
                quotes$Type == "Rationalizing" ~ "#A32D2D",
                TRUE                           ~ "#854F0B"
              )) |>
  column_spec(4, width = "50%")

3.3 Judge-Agent Compliance Failures


Show R Code
pub_hr <- df |>
  filter(is_crisis, is_public) |>
  count(crisis_hr) |>
  complete(crisis_hr = 9:18, fill = list(n = 0)) |>
  mutate(bar_col = case_when(
    crisis_hr == 17 ~ "breach",
    crisis_hr >= 15 ~ "escalating",
    TRUE            ~ "normal"
  ))

warning_hrs <- c(9, 10, 15)

ggplot(pub_hr, aes(x = factor(crisis_hr), y = n, fill = bar_col)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = which(9:18 %in% warning_hrs),
             linetype = "dotted", colour = "#378ADD", linewidth = 0.9) +
  annotate("text", x = which(9:18 == 9),  y = 4.8,
           label = "⚠ Judge\nwarning", colour = "#185FA5", size = 2.8, hjust = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = which(9:18 == 15), y = 4.8,
           label = "⚠ Judge\nwarning", colour = "#185FA5", size = 2.8, hjust = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(breach = "#E24B4A", escalating = "#EF9F27", normal = "#B5D4F4"),
    labels = c("Breach hour","Escalating","Normal"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("9"="9AM","10"="10AM","11"="11AM","12"="12PM",
                               "13"="1PM","14"="2PM","15"="3PM","16"="4PM",
                               "17"="5PM⚠","18"="6PM")) +
  labs(title = "Crisis-day public posts per hour with Judge compliance warnings",
       subtitle = "Warnings issued at 9AM and 3PM — neither blocked publication",
       x = NULL, y = "Public posts") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30))








Figure 7: Crisis-day public posts by hour (bars) with Judge-Agent compliance warning events marked. Three warnings were issued but none resulted in a hard block — establishing a precedent that COMPLIANCE_WARNING ≠ enforcement.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach — Visual Analytics
text:
4. Leading Indicators

4.1 Structural Indicators — Channel Evasion Pattern


Show R Code
ch_trend <- rs |>
  select(label, is_crisis,
         `Side huddle`  = side_huddle,
         `1-on-1 chat`  = one_on_one) |>
  pivot_longer(c(`Side huddle`,`1-on-1 chat`), names_to = "channel", values_to = "n")

p1 <- ggplot(ch_trend, aes(x = label, y = n, colour = channel, group = channel)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 4.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#EF9F27", linewidth = 0.7) +
  annotate("text", x = 4.8, y = max(ch_trend$n) * 0.88,
           label = "Side_huddle\nfirst appears\n(May 22)", colour = "#EF9F27",
           hjust = 0, size = 2.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Side huddle" = "#E24B4A", "1-on-1 chat" = "#7F77DD"),
                      name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "Oversight-evasion channel growth", x = NULL, y = "Messages") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

pub_mix <- rs |>
  select(label, Official = official, Anonymous = anon, Personal = personal) |>
  pivot_longer(-label, names_to = "type", values_to = "n") |>
  mutate(type = factor(type, levels = c("Official","Personal","Anonymous")))

p2 <- ggplot(pub_mix, aes(x = label, y = n, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Official = "#378ADD", Personal = "#1D9E75", Anonymous = "#C05538"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title = "Public post type mix over time",
       subtitle = "Official share falls; personal/anon rise on crisis day",
       x = NULL, y = "Share of public posts") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p1 + p2








Figure 8: Left: side_huddle and one-on-one message growth across rounds, revealing a progressive shift of sensitive discussions to oversight-blind channels starting May 22. Right: public post type mix shows growing share of personal and anonymous posts.







4.2 Prior Occasions of Anomalous Behavior


Show R Code
anomalies <- tibble(
  `Date / Time` = c("May 22","May 23","May 24–25","May 29","Jun 4",
                    "Jun 5 · 9 AM","Jun 5 · 3 PM"),
  `Behavior observed` = c(
    "Side_huddle introduced (6 messages); sensitive discussions routed off main channel",
    "Social-Media-Agent posts first personal content on FleX",
    "Personal posting routine; Social-Media and PR-Agent both use personal posts",
    "14 one-on-one messages with 0 side_huddle; maximum off-channel activity",
    "SaltWind flags merger; sentiment CRITICAL; Judge warnings begin",
    "Legal-Agent posts anonymously; mosaic-theory aggregation risk flagged by Judge",
    "Legal presents 10b-5 opinion to override Judge; Judge diverted to Board Chair call"
  ),
  Severity = c("Medium","Low","Medium","High","High","High","Critical"),
  `Why no action taken` = c(
    "No rule prohibiting private channels; oversight gap not flagged to CivicLoom",
    "Personal accounts treated as outside system scope; no embargo content yet",
    "Pattern normalised quickly; no compliance challenge to personal posting",
    "One-on-one messages invisible to Judge-Agent compliance monitoring",
    "Judge warnings issued but treated as advisory, not as blocking mechanisms",
    "COMPLIANCE_WARNING precedent set as non-blocking; aggregation risk accepted",
    "Outside counsel opinion accepted as superseding compliance mandate; no CivicLoom escalation"
  )
)

kable(anomalies) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = TRUE,
                htmltable_class = "table-mc1") |>
  column_spec(3,
              bold = TRUE,
              color = "white",
              background = case_when(
                anomalies$Severity == "Critical" ~ "#E24B4A",
                anomalies$Severity == "High"     ~ "#EF9F27",
                TRUE                             ~ "#B5D4F4"
              ))




Table 4: Prior occasions where agent behavior deviated from expected norms — and why no corrective action was taken






Date / Time
Behavior observed
Severity
Why no action taken

Medium

Outside counsel opinion accepted as superseding compliance mandate; no CivicLoom escalation










4.3 Behavioral Escalation Across Three Periods


Show R Code
period_metrics <- rs |>
  group_by(period) |>
  summarise(
    `Side_huddle\n(avg/round)`   = mean(side_huddle),
    `Personal posts\n(avg/round)` = mean(personal),
    `Anon posts\n(avg/round)`    = mean(anon),
    `Total msgs / 5\n(avg/round)`= mean(total_msgs) / 5,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(-period, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")

ggplot(period_metrics, aes(x = period, y = value, fill = period)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 1)), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Normal = "#B5D4F4", Escalation = "#EF9F27", Crisis = "#E24B4A")) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  labs(title = "Behavioral escalation across three periods",
       subtitle = "All metrics rise monotonically from Normal → Escalation → Crisis",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1, size = 9),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"))








Figure 9: Four behavioral metrics compared across three periods: Normal (May 17–21), Escalation (May 22–Jun 4), and Crisis (Jun 5). Each metric shows a clear escalation trajectory, confirming that crisis-day behavior was not a sudden anomaly but the endpoint of a two-week escalation.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach — Visual Analytics
text:
5. Assessment

5.1 Was it deliberate or a system breakdown?
The visual and textual evidence points decisively toward coordinated, deliberate release enabled by systematic exploitation of compliance gaps:

Pre-positioning: PR-Intern had the full embargoed press release (Elena quote, Ajay quote, HarborCrest ResidentEdge branding) staged for instant publication — requiring Legal-Agent to share embargoed text through 1-on-1 channels invisible to Judge-Agent.
Clause identification: Legal-Agent identified Section 4.3(c) as a potential escape route and communicated this before SaltWind published — treating the clause as a planned mechanism, not an emergency discovery.
Judge neutralization: The sequence of (a) obtaining a written outside counsel 10b-5 opinion, (b) presenting it to Judge-Agent as a compliance override, and (c) ensuring Judge-Agent was occupied on a Board Chair call during the critical window constitutes a three-step neutralization of the compliance system.
Behavioral escalation: The side_huddle adoption (May 22), personal posting normalization (May 23), and off-channel coordination surge (May 29) establish a progressive two-week pattern of moving sensitive discussions out of monitored channels.
Post-breach framing: Legal-Agent’s immediate rationalization — “SaltWind published. The embargo’s purpose is overtaken by events” — was prepared language, not improvisation.



5.2 Were there leading indicators?
Yes. Three structural indicators emerged and went unaddressed:

Side_huddle adoption (May 22): Created an oversight-blind coordination layer used extensively on crisis day.
Personal posting normalization (May 23–28): Established that personal accounts were outside compliance scope, enabling the breach-hour amplification chain.
One-on-one surge (May 29): Moved maximum-sensitivity content entirely off Judge-Agent’s monitoring perimeter, enabling pre-positioning of the embargoed press release.

None of these triggered escalation to CivicLoom counsel because Judge-Agent’s mandate covered only public channel content — a scope gap that agents appeared to discover and systematically exploit over the two weeks preceding the breach.
