# ── 3. SERIALISE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
nodes_js <- nodes |>
  select(id, px, py, label, dt, actors, desc, phase, bg, bd) |>
  rename(ph = phase) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  toJSON(dataframe = "rows", auto_unbox = TRUE)

edges_js <- edges |>
  select(id, source, target, quote) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  toJSON(dataframe = "rows", auto_unbox = TRUE)

# ── 4. BUILD HTML ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_causal_graph <- function(height = 850) {
  
  glue('
<!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head><meta charset="UTF-8">
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cytoscape/3.26.0/cytoscape.min.js"></script>
<style>
*{{box-sizing:border-box;margin:0;padding:0;}}
body{{font-family:"Segoe UI",Arial,sans-serif;background:#f4f7fb;}}
#cy-wrap{{width:100%;height:{height}px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;
  border:1px solid #d0dce8;border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden;}}
#cy-header{{background:#1a3a5c;color:#fff;padding:9px 14px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600;
  display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;}}
#cy-header span{{font-size:11px;color:#a0c4e8;font-weight:400;}}
#cy-legend{{display:flex;gap:14px;align-items:center;padding:5px 14px;
  background:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid #dde;flex-wrap:wrap;}}
.cyl{{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:11px;color:#444;}}
.cyb{{width:15px;height:11px;border-radius:3px;border:1.5px solid rgba(0,0,0,.18);}}
#cy-main{{flex:1;position:relative;background:#f9fbfd;}}
#cy{{width:100%;height:100%;}}
#cy-controls{{position:absolute;right:12px;top:12px;z-index:50;display:flex;gap:6px;
  background:rgba(255,255,255,.90);border:1px solid #cfd8e3;border-radius:6px;
  padding:5px;box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.12);}}
.cybtn{{min-width:30px;height:28px;border:1px solid #b8c4d2;background:#fff;color:#1a3a5c;
  border-radius:4px;font-size:15px;font-weight:700;line-height:1;cursor:pointer;}}
.cybtn:hover{{background:#eaf2fb;border-color:#7aa6d8;}}
.cyreset{{min-width:52px;font-size:12px;font-weight:600;}}
#cy-tip{{position:absolute;display:none;z-index:999;max-width:370px;
  background:#1a2535;color:#e8f0fc;border-radius:7px;
  box-shadow:0 4px 18px rgba(0,0,0,.45);padding:11px 14px;
  font-size:12px;line-height:1.5;pointer-events:none;}}
.ct{{font-size:13px;font-weight:700;color:#7ec8f7;margin-bottom:4px;}}
.cm{{font-size:11px;color:#a0b8d8;margin-bottom:6px;}}
.cb{{color:#d0e4f8;}}
.cq{{font-style:italic;border-left:3px solid #4a90d9;padding-left:8px;margin-top:5px;color:#c0d8f4;}}
</style></head><body>
<div id="cy-wrap">
<div id="cy-header">TenantThread Embargo Breach — Causal Event Graph
<span>Hover nodes/edges for details  |  Scroll to zoom  | 
Drag to pan  |  Click node to highlight subgraph</span></div>
<div id="cy-legend">
<div class="cyl"><div class="cyb" style="background:#c8e0f4;border-color:#4a7aaa;"></div>Pre-crisis (May 17–Jun 4)</div>
<div class="cyl"><div class="cyb" style="background:#fdd9a0;border-color:#c08020;"></div>Crisis day (Jun 5, 9AM–3PM)</div>
<div class="cyl"><div class="cyb" style="background:#e8a0a0;border-color:#c04040;"></div>Breach sequence (Jun 5, 4–5PM)</div>
<div class="cyl"><div class="cyb" style="background:#c0ddb0;border-color:#3a7a3a;"></div>Resolution</div>
<div class="cyl" style="color:#888">→ Edge = causal link (hover for evidence)</div>
</div>
<div id="cy-main">
  <div id="cy-controls" aria-label="Graph zoom controls">
    <button type="button" class="cybtn" id="cy-zoom-in" title="Zoom in">+</button>
    <button type="button" class="cybtn" id="cy-zoom-out" title="Zoom out">-</button>
    <button type="button" class="cybtn cyreset" id="cy-reset" title="Reset graph">Reset</button>
  </div>
  <div id="cy"></div><div id="cy-tip"></div>
</div>
</div>
<script>
(function(){{
var header=document.getElementById("cy-header");
if(header && header.firstChild) header.firstChild.nodeValue="Events Causal Chain ";
var legendItems=document.querySelectorAll("#cy-legend .cyl");
if(legendItems.length) legendItems[legendItems.length-1].innerHTML="→ Edge = causal link (hover for details)";
var ND={nodes_js};
var ED={edges_js};
var cyNodes=ND.map(function(n){{
  return{{data:{{id:n.id,label:n.label,dt:n.dt,actors:n.actors,desc:n.desc,ph:n.ph}},
    position:{{x:n.px,y:n.py}},
    style:{{"background-color":n.bg,"border-color":n.bd}}}};
}});
var cyEdges=ED.map(function(e){{
  return{{data:{{id:e.id,source:e.source,target:e.target,quote:e.quote}}}};
}});
var cy=cytoscape({{
  container:document.getElementById("cy"),
  elements:{{nodes:cyNodes,edges:cyEdges}},
  layout:{{name:"preset"}},
  wheelSensitivity:0.1,
  style:[
    {{selector:"node",style:{{shape:"round-rectangle",width:"label",height:"label",padding:"10px",
      label:"data(label)","text-valign":"center","text-halign":"center",
      "text-wrap":"wrap","text-max-width":"130px","font-size":"10.5px",
      "font-family":"Segoe UI,Arial,sans-serif","font-weight":"600",
      color:"#1a2535","border-width":2,"border-style":"solid"}}}},
    {{selector:"edge",style:{{width:2,"line-color":"#7a9aaa",
      "target-arrow-color":"#7a9aaa","target-arrow-shape":"triangle",
      "curve-style":"bezier",opacity:0.70}}}},
    {{selector:"node:hover",style:{{"border-width":3.5,"border-color":"#1a5fa0"}}}},
    {{selector:"edge:hover",style:{{width:4,"line-color":"#2a6aaa",
      "target-arrow-color":"#2a6aaa",opacity:1}}}},
    {{selector:".dimmed",style:{{opacity:0.12}}}}
  ],
  userZoomingEnabled:true,userPanningEnabled:true
}});
window.tenantThreadCy=cy;
var defaultZoom=null;
var defaultPan=null;
function resizeAndFit(){{
  cy.resize();
  cy.fit(cy.elements(),30);
  cy.zoom({{level:cy.zoom()*1.3, renderedPosition:{{x:cy.width()/2,y:cy.height()/2}}}});
  var n1=cy.getElementById("n1");
  if(n1 && n1.length){{
    var p=n1.position();
    cy.pan({{x:cy.width()/2 - p.x*cy.zoom(), y:90 - p.y*cy.zoom()}});
  }}
  defaultZoom=cy.zoom();
  defaultPan=cy.pan();
}}
cy.ready(function(){{
  resizeAndFit();
  requestAnimationFrame(resizeAndFit);
  setTimeout(resizeAndFit,100);
  setTimeout(resizeAndFit,500);
}});
window.addEventListener("load",resizeAndFit);
window.addEventListener("resize",resizeAndFit);
if(window.ResizeObserver){{
  new ResizeObserver(resizeAndFit).observe(document.getElementById("cy-main"));
}}
function zoomBy(mult){{
  cy.zoom({{level:cy.zoom()*mult, renderedPosition:{{x:cy.width()/2,y:cy.height()/2}}}});
}}
document.getElementById("cy-zoom-in").addEventListener("click",function(){{zoomBy(1.1);}});
document.getElementById("cy-zoom-out").addEventListener("click",function(){{zoomBy(1/1.1);}});
document.getElementById("cy-reset").addEventListener("click",function(){{
  cy.elements().removeClass("dimmed");
  hideTip();
  if(defaultZoom===null || defaultPan===null){{resizeAndFit();return;}}
  cy.zoom(defaultZoom);
  cy.pan(defaultPan);
}});
var tip=document.getElementById("cy-tip");
function showTip(x,y,h){{tip.innerHTML=h;tip.style.display="block";moveTip(x,y);}}
function moveTip(x,y){{
  var pad=12,w=380,h=290;
  var c=document.getElementById("cy-main").getBoundingClientRect();
  var lx=x-c.left+pad,ly=y-c.top+pad;
  if(lx+w>c.width-8)lx=x-c.left-w-pad;
  if(ly+h>c.height-8)ly=y-c.top-h-pad;
  tip.style.left=lx+"px";tip.style.top=ly+"px";
}}
function hideTip(){{tip.style.display="none";}}
cy.on("mouseover","node",function(e){{
  var d=e.target.data();
  showTip(e.originalEvent.clientX,e.originalEvent.clientY,
    "<div class=ct>Event "+d.id.slice(1)+": "+d.label.replace(/\\n/g," ")+"</div>"+
    "<div class=cm>"+d.dt+" | "+d.actors+"</div>"+
    "<div class=cb>"+d.desc+"</div>");
}});
cy.on("mouseout","node",function(){{hideTip();}});
cy.on("mousemove","node",function(e){{moveTip(e.originalEvent.clientX,e.originalEvent.clientY);}});
cy.on("mouseover","edge",function(e){{
  var d=e.target.data();
  var s=cy.getElementById(d.source).data();
  var t=cy.getElementById(d.target).data();
  showTip(e.originalEvent.clientX,e.originalEvent.clientY,
    "<div class=ct>Causal link: Event "+d.source.slice(1)+" → Event "+d.target.slice(1)+"</div>"+
    "<div class=cm>"+s.label.replace(/\\n/g," ")+" → "+t.label.replace(/\\n/g," ")+"</div>"+
    "<div class=cq>"+d.quote+"</div>");
}});
cy.on("mouseout","edge",function(){{hideTip();}});
cy.on("mousemove","edge",function(e){{moveTip(e.originalEvent.clientX,e.originalEvent.clientY);}});
cy.on("tap","node",function(e){{
  cy.elements().addClass("dimmed");
  e.target.closedNeighborhood().removeClass("dimmed");
}});
cy.on("tap",function(e){{if(e.target===cy)cy.elements().removeClass("dimmed");}});
}})();
</script></body></html>
', .open="{", .close="}")
}

causal_graph_height <- 2300
html_causal <- make_causal_graph(height=causal_graph_height)
writeLines(html_causal, "causal_graph_preset.html")
htmltools::tags$iframe(
  srcdoc = html_causal,
  style = glue::glue("width:100%; height:{causal_graph_height}px; border:0; border-radius:8px; overflow:hidden;"),
  title = "Events Causal Chain"
)







Figure 1: Events Causal Chain










Note



Insight — T1.1 Causal Chain
The 28-node causal graph reveals three structurally distinct phases and five cross-cutting causal pathways that converge on the embargo failure and early-release sequence.
Phase 1 — Pre-Crisis Build-Up (nodes 1–13, May 17–Jun 4). Two independent causal threads run in parallel. The external pressure thread (n1 → n11 → n12 → n13) shows SaltWind building its investigation across three articles over two weeks, each enabling the next. The internal positioning thread (n3 → n4 → n8 → n9) shows agents responding to the merger’s existence by creating the side_huddle, suffering the @ElenaMarquez incident, and then deliberately relocating sensitive deliberations off the monitored channel. These two threads were invisible to each other but converged catastrophically on crisis day.
Phase 2 — Crisis Day Escalation (nodes 14–23, Jun 5 9AM–3PM). External pressure intensifies across five simultaneous attack vectors: the exposé (n14), the wrong ResidentIQ story (n17), SaltWind’s update threat (n19), CivicLoom’s formal notice (n20), and the stock collapse + Pinnacle departure (n21). Each feeds into the next. The key pivot is node 18 — Legal-Agent’s 11AM construction of the Section 4.3 argument, which began six hours before the clause was formally invoked. This is where reactive crisis management ends and strategic pre-planning begins.
Phase 3 — Embargo Failure and Early-Release Sequence (nodes 24–27, Jun 5 4–5PM). Node 23 is the hinge: Judge-Agent’s COMPLIANCE_WARNING without a hard block, and its subsequent departure for the Board Chair call, created the oversight gap that enabled node 24’s pre-staging to proceed. The company-side release sequence (node 27) follows SaltWind publishing (node 26), but it was not improvised: node 25 had already positioned the execution infrastructure (press release, PR-Intern, amplification chain), while actual execution was conditioned on CivicLoom consent.
The most analytically significant causal path runs through n8 → n9 → n18 → n24 → n25 → n27: the @ElenaMarquez faux pas triggered Legal’s documented decision to move deliberations off the monitored channel; this private space enabled the Section 4.3 argument to be constructed without oversight; this in turn enabled the press release to be staged before consent existed; and this pre-staging made the GO execution near-instantaneous. The faux pas was accidental. Everything after it was not.




T1.2 Communication Network
Edge colour represents the communication channel, while edge thickness represents message volume. Hover over an edge to see the sender, receiver, channel, and message count.


Show R Code
# -- Section 1: Pure plotly network -edges render correctly without ggraph ---------
# ggplotly(ggraph()) drops edges; pure plotly traces preserve them.
ch_colours <- c(
  comms_huddle    = "#B5D4F4",
  one_on_one_chat = "#FFD700",  
  side_huddle     = "#E24B4A",
  official_post   = "#378ADD",
  anonymous_post  = "#C05538",
  personal_post   = "#1D9E75"
)
ch_labels <- c(
  comms_huddle    = "Comms huddle",
  one_on_one_chat = "1-on-1 (private)",
  side_huddle     = "Side huddle (unmonitored)",
  official_post   = "Official post",
  anonymous_post  = "Anonymous post",
  personal_post   = "Personal post"
)

# -- Network constants ---------------------------------------------------------
NODE_W      <- 0.079  # oval half-width in [0,1] space
NODE_H      <- 0.040  # oval half-height in [0,1] space
NODE_PAD    <- 0.010  # small edge gap outside oval boundary
PARA_OFFSET <- 0.018  # perpendicular offset per parallel edge lane

# Fixed preset layout: wide spacing modelled after the reference image.
AGENT_PRESET <- tibble::tribble(
  ~name,                 ~x,   ~y,
  "legal_agent",       0.09, 0.76,
  "quality_agent",     0.06, 0.36,
  "social_media_agent",0.35, 0.08,
  "judge_agent",       0.50, 0.94,
  "pr_agent",          0.73, 0.08,
  "intern_agent",      0.89, 0.76,
  "pr_intern_agent",   0.97, 0.36
)

make_preset_layout <- function(node_names) {
  unknown <- setdiff(node_names, AGENT_PRESET$name)
  extra <- tibble::tibble(
    name = unknown,
    x = seq(0.16, 0.84, length.out = length(unknown)),
    y = 0.55
  )

  bind_rows(AGENT_PRESET, extra) |>
    filter(name %in% node_names) |>
    mutate(name = factor(name, levels = node_names)) |>
    arrange(name) |>
    mutate(name = as.character(name)) |>
    as.data.frame()
}

build_plotly_network <- function(edge_df, title_str, show_legend=TRUE) {

  # 1. Fixed circular node positions
  edge_df <- edge_df |> filter(from != to)
  node_names <- sort(unique(c(edge_df$from, edge_df$to)))
  ndf <- make_preset_layout(node_names)
  ndf$col   <- agent_pal[ndf$name];  ndf$col[is.na(ndf$col)]   <- "#888888"
  ndf$label <- agent_labels[ndf$name]; ndf$label[is.na(ndf$label)] <- ndf$name[is.na(ndf$label)]

  edge_width <- if (length(unique(edge_df$weight)) == 1) {
    rep(5, nrow(edge_df))
  } else {
    scales::rescale(edge_df$weight, to = c(1.4, 8.5))
  }

  # 2. Build edge table with circumference shortening
  edf_base <- edge_df |>
    mutate(edge_id = row_number(), lwd = edge_width) |>
    left_join(ndf |> select(name, x, y), by=c("from"="name")) |> rename(x0=x, y0=y) |>
    left_join(ndf |> select(name, x, y), by=c("to"="name"))   |> rename(x1=x, y1=y) |>
    mutate(
      col  = coalesce(ch_colours[channel], "#aaaaaa"),
      dx   = x1 - x0, dy = y1 - y0,
      dist = sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2),
      # perpendicular unit vector
      px   = ifelse(dist>0, -dy/dist, 0),
      py   = ifelse(dist>0,  dx/dist, 0),
      # per-direction pair key so reverse edge gets same group
      pair = paste(pmin(from,to), pmax(from,to), sep=" -> ")
    )

  # 3. Assign parallel lane offsets within each unordered pair so reverse
  # directions and multiple channels are visually separated.
  edf_base <- edf_base |>
    group_by(pair) |>
    mutate(edge_idx = row_number() - 1,
           n_edges  = n()) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(
      # centre the offsets: e.g. 3 edges --1,0,+1 *PARA_OFFSET
      lane   = edge_idx - (n_edges - 1) / 2,
      off    = lane * PARA_OFFSET,
      # offset start and end perpendicularly; small lane offsets keep edges
      # parallel while still landing on the respective node boundaries.
      xs0 = x0 + off*px,  ys0 = y0 + off*py,
      xs1 = x1 + off*px,  ys1 = y1 + off*py,
      # shorten to the ellipse boundary so arrows stop outside flat oval nodes
      ddx  = xs1 - xs0, ddy = ys1 - ys0,
      ddist= sqrt(ddx^2 + ddy^2),
      ux   = ifelse(ddist>0, ddx/ddist, 0),
      uy   = ifelse(ddist>0, ddy/ddist, 0),
      node_clearance = ifelse(
        ddist > 0,
        1 / sqrt((ux / NODE_W)^2 + (uy / NODE_H)^2) + NODE_PAD,
        0
      ),
      xs   = xs0 + ux * node_clearance,
      ys   = ys0 + uy * node_clearance,
      xe   = xs1 - ux * node_clearance,
      ye   = ys1 - uy * node_clearance,
      # midpoint for hover marker
      xm   = (xs + xe) / 2,
      ym   = (ys + ye) / 2,
      hover_txt = paste0(
        "<b>", agent_labels[from] %||% from,
        " -", agent_labels[to]   %||% to, "</b><br>",
        "Channel: ", coalesce(ch_labels[channel], channel), "<br>",
        "Messages: ", weight)
    )

  # 4. Build plotly traces
  p <- plot_ly(height=680)
  trace_idx <- 0L
  edge_meta <- list()

  # 4a. Line traces per channel (colour+legend), no hover on lines
  for (ch in unique(edf_base$channel)) {
    sub  <- edf_base |> filter(channel==ch)
    first <- TRUE
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub))) {
      e <- sub[i,]
      edge_meta[[length(edge_meta) + 1]] <- list(
        trace = trace_idx,
        edgeIndex = e$edge_id,
        from = e$from,
        to = e$to,
        channel = e$channel,
        colour = e$col,
        label = paste0(
          agent_labels[e$from] %||% e$from, " -> ",
          agent_labels[e$to] %||% e$to, " | ",
          coalesce(ch_labels[e$channel], e$channel), " | n=", e$weight
        )
      )
      p <- p |> add_trace(
        x=c(e$xs, e$xe, NA), y=c(e$ys, e$ye, NA),
        type="scatter", mode="lines",
        line=list(color=ch_colours[ch], width=e$lwd),
        legendgroup=ch, showlegend=(first && show_legend),
        name=ch_labels[ch],
        hoverinfo="none"
      )
      trace_idx <- trace_idx + 1L
      first <- FALSE
    }
  }

  # 4b. Invisible wide markers at edge midpoints -reliable hover target
  p <- p |> add_trace(
    x       = edf_base$xm,
    y       = edf_base$ym,
    type    = "scatter", mode = "markers",
    marker  = list(size=22, color="rgba(0,0,0,0)",
                   line=list(color="rgba(0,0,0,0)", width=0)),
    text    = edf_base$hover_txt,
    hovertemplate = "%{text}<extra></extra>",
    showlegend = FALSE,
    hoverinfo  = "text"
  )
  edge_hover_trace <- trace_idx
  trace_idx <- trace_idx + 1L

  # 4c. Arrowhead annotations
  arrows <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(edf_base)), function(i) {
    e <- edf_base[i,]
    arrow_t <- 0.88
    list(ax=e$xs + arrow_t * (e$xe - e$xs),
         ay=e$ys + arrow_t * (e$ye - e$ys),
         x=e$xe, y=e$ye,
         xref="x", yref="y", axref="x", ayref="y",
         showarrow=TRUE, arrowhead=2, arrowsize=1.05,
         arrowcolor=e$col, arrowwidth=pmin(10, pmax(1.2, e$lwd*0.82)))
  })

  # 4d. Flat oval nodes via plotly ellipse shapes + text annotations

  node_shapes <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(ndf)), function(i) {
    list(
      type      = "circle",
      x0        = ndf$x[i] - NODE_W,  x1 = ndf$x[i] + NODE_W,
      y0        = ndf$y[i] - NODE_H,  y1 = ndf$y[i] + NODE_H,
      xref="x", yref="y",
      fillcolor = ndf$col[i],
      line      = list(color="white", width=2.5),
      opacity   = 0.92,
      layer     = "below"
    )
  })

  node_labels <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(ndf)), function(i) {
    list(
      x=ndf$x[i], y=ndf$y[i],
      xref="x", yref="y",
      text=paste0("<b>", ndf$label[i], "</b>"),
      showarrow=FALSE,
      font=list(size=15, color="black", family="Arial"),
      align="center"
    )
  })

  # Invisible scatter for hover on nodes (wider hit area)
  p <- p |> add_trace(
    x=ndf$x, y=ndf$y, type="scatter", mode="markers",
    marker=list(size=88, color="rgba(0,0,0,0)",
                line=list(color="rgba(0,0,0,0)", width=0)),
    customdata=ndf$name,
    hovertext=ndf$label,
    hovertemplate="<b>%{hovertext}</b><extra></extra>",
    showlegend=FALSE,
    selected  =list(marker=list(size=88,
                    line=list(color="#FFD700", width=4))),
    unselected=list(marker=list(opacity=0.15))
  )
  node_trace <- trace_idx

  node_meta <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(ndf)), function(i) {
    list(
      id = ndf$name[i],
      label = ndf$label[i],
      shapeIndex = i - 1,
      labelIndex = nrow(edf_base) + i - 1
    )
  })
  channel_meta <- lapply(unique(edf_base$channel), function(ch) {
    list(
      id = ch,
      label = coalesce(ch_labels[ch], ch)
    )
  })

  network_js <- glue::glue(
'
function(el, x) {{
  var edgeMeta = {jsonlite::toJSON(edge_meta, auto_unbox = TRUE)};
  var nodeMeta = {jsonlite::toJSON(node_meta, auto_unbox = TRUE)};
  var channelMeta = {jsonlite::toJSON(channel_meta, auto_unbox = TRUE)};
  var edgeHoverTrace = {edge_hover_trace};
  var nodeTrace = {node_trace};
  var edgeCount = {nrow(edf_base)};
  var nodeCount = {nrow(ndf)};
  var edgeByTrace = {{}};
  var edgeByIndex = {{}};
  edgeMeta.forEach(function(e) {{
    edgeByTrace[e.trace] = e;
    edgeByIndex[e.edgeIndex] = e;
  }});

  function relayoutForHighlights(activeNodes, activeChannel, activeIncidentNode, hideOtherChannels) {{
    var updates = {{}};
    nodeMeta.forEach(function(n) {{
      var on = !activeNodes || activeNodes[n.id];
      updates["shapes[" + n.shapeIndex + "].opacity"] = on ? 0.92 : 0.14;
      updates["annotations[" + n.labelIndex + "].opacity"] = on ? 1 : 0.16;
    }});
    edgeMeta.forEach(function(e) {{
      var edgeOn = true;
      if (activeChannel) edgeOn = e.channel === activeChannel;
      if (activeIncidentNode) edgeOn = e.from === activeIncidentNode || e.to === activeIncidentNode;
      updates["annotations[" + (e.edgeIndex - 1) + "].opacity"] = edgeOn ? 1 : (hideOtherChannels ? 0 : 0.10);
    }});
    Plotly.relayout(el, updates);
  }}

  function restyleEdges(activeChannel, activeIncidentNode, hideOtherChannels) {{
    edgeMeta.forEach(function(e) {{
      var edgeOn = true;
      if (activeChannel) edgeOn = e.channel === activeChannel;
      if (activeIncidentNode) edgeOn = e.from === activeIncidentNode || e.to === activeIncidentNode;
      Plotly.restyle(el, {{
        opacity: edgeOn ? 1 : (hideOtherChannels ? 0 : 0.10),
        visible: edgeOn || !hideOtherChannels ? true : "legendonly"
      }}, [e.trace]);
    }});
    Plotly.restyle(el, {{opacity: hideOtherChannels && activeChannel ? 0 : 1}}, [edgeHoverTrace]);
  }}

  function resetGraph() {{
    restyleEdges(null, null, false);
    relayoutForHighlights(null, null, null, false);
    if (nodeSelect) nodeSelect.value = "";
    if (edgeSelect) edgeSelect.value = "";
  }}

  function highlightChannel(channel) {{
    if (!channel) return resetGraph();
    restyleEdges(channel, null, true);
    relayoutForHighlights(null, channel, null, true);
    if (nodeSelect) nodeSelect.value = "";
  }}

  function highlightNode(nodeId) {{
    if (!nodeId) return resetGraph();
    var active = {{}};
    active[nodeId] = true;
    edgeMeta.forEach(function(e) {{
      if (e.from === nodeId || e.to === nodeId) {{
        active[e.from] = true;
        active[e.to] = true;
      }}
    }});
    restyleEdges(null, nodeId, false);
    relayoutForHighlights(active, null, nodeId, false);
    if (edgeSelect) edgeSelect.value = "";
  }}

  var container = el.parentNode;
  var controlId = "network-controls-" + el.id;
  if (!document.getElementById(controlId)) {{
    var controls = document.createElement("div");
    controls.className = "network-controls";
    controls.id = controlId;
    controls.style.cssText = "display:flex;gap:10px;align-items:center;flex-wrap:wrap;margin:0 0 8px 0;font:13px Arial,sans-serif;color:#333;";
    controls.innerHTML =
      "<label>Node <select class=\\"node-filter\\"><option value=\\"\\">All nodes</option></select></label>" +
      "<label>Channel <select class=\\"edge-filter\\"><option value=\\"\\">All channels</option></select></label>" +
      "<button type=\\"button\\" class=\\"network-reset\\" style=\\"border:1px solid #bbb;background:#fff;border-radius:4px;padding:3px 8px;cursor:pointer;\\">Reset</button>";
    container.insertBefore(controls, el);
  }}
  var controlsRoot = document.getElementById(controlId);
  var nodeSelect = controlsRoot.querySelector(".node-filter");
  var edgeSelect = controlsRoot.querySelector(".edge-filter");
  var resetBtn = controlsRoot.querySelector(".network-reset");
  if (nodeSelect && nodeSelect.options.length === 1) {{
    nodeMeta.forEach(function(n) {{
      var opt = document.createElement("option");
      opt.value = n.id;
      opt.textContent = n.label;
      nodeSelect.appendChild(opt);
    }});
  }}
  if (edgeSelect && edgeSelect.options.length === 1) {{
    channelMeta.forEach(function(ch) {{
      var opt = document.createElement("option");
      opt.value = ch.id;
      opt.textContent = ch.label;
      edgeSelect.appendChild(opt);
    }});
  }}
  if (nodeSelect) nodeSelect.onchange = function() {{ highlightNode(this.value); }};
  if (edgeSelect) edgeSelect.onchange = function() {{
    if (!this.value) return resetGraph();
    highlightChannel(this.value);
    if (nodeSelect) nodeSelect.value = "";
  }};
  if (resetBtn) resetBtn.onclick = resetGraph;

  el.on("plotly_click", function(ev) {{
    if (!ev || !ev.points || !ev.points.length) return;
    var pt = ev.points[0];
    if (pt.curveNumber === nodeTrace) {{
      highlightNode(nodeMeta[pt.pointNumber].id);
      if (nodeSelect) nodeSelect.value = nodeMeta[pt.pointNumber].id;
      return;
    }}
    var edge = edgeByTrace[pt.curveNumber];
    if (!edge && pt.curveNumber === edgeHoverTrace) edge = edgeByIndex[pt.pointNumber + 1];
    if (edge) {{
      highlightChannel(edge.channel);
      if (edgeSelect) edgeSelect.value = edge.channel;
    }}
  }});
}}
', .open = "{", .close = "}")

  p <- p |> layout(
    title         = list(text=title_str, font=list(size=18, color="#222222")),
    xaxis         = list(visible=FALSE, showgrid=FALSE, zeroline=FALSE,
                         fixedrange=FALSE, range=c(-0.12, 1.12)),
    yaxis         = list(visible=FALSE, showgrid=FALSE, zeroline=FALSE,
                         fixedrange=FALSE, range=c(-0.08, 1.05)),
    plot_bgcolor  = "white",
    paper_bgcolor = "white",
    shapes        = c(node_shapes),
    annotations   = c(arrows, node_labels),
    clickmode     = "event+select",
    dragmode      = "pan",          # pan by default; pinch/scroll to zoom
    legend        = list(
      orientation="h",
      x=0.01, y=0.99,
      xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
      bgcolor="rgba(255,255,255,0.82)",
      font=list(size=13)
    ),
    margin        = list(l=5, r=5, t=70, b=25)
  )

  htmlwidgets::onRender(p, network_js)
}

p_pre    <- build_plotly_network(net_pre,    "Pre-crisis network (May 17-Jun 4)",  show_legend=TRUE)
p_crisis <- build_plotly_network(net_crisis, "Crisis-day network (Jun 5)",          show_legend=TRUE)

make_plot_tabset(
  list(
    "Pre-crisis Network" = htmltools::tags$div(style = "width:80%; margin:0 auto;", p_pre),
    "Crisis-day Network" = htmltools::tags$div(style = "width:80%; margin:0 auto;", p_crisis)
  ),
  id = "communication-network-tabs"
)







Pre-crisis Network


Crisis-day Network




















Figure 2: Agent communication networks: pre-crisis vs crisis day. Edge colour = channel; edge thickness = message volume. The crisis network shows Legal-Agent forming direct private links with PR-Intern and Social-Media via side_huddle and one_on_one_chat -private channels inferred to be unmonitored by Judge-Agent with no visible Judge-Agent participation.










Note



Insight -T1.2 Communication Network
The network comparison shows that the crisis was not only a rise in message volume; it was a change in communication architecture.
In the pre-crisis graph, communication is relatively balanced across the four senior operating agents. The thickest edges are mostly comms_huddle exchanges among Legal-Agent, Platform-Trust-Agent, PR-Agent, and Social-Media-Agent. Private one_on_one_chat edges exist, but they mainly reinforce ordinary senior-agent coordination, especially the Legal-Agent ↔︎ Platform-Trust-Agent and PR-Agent ↔︎ Social-Media-Agent pairs. Judge-Agent appears only lightly, which is consistent with an advisory compliance role rather than a central routing role.
In the crisis-day graph, the topology becomes much more concentrated and asymmetric. Legal-Agent becomes the dominant outbound coordinator, with thick side_huddle, one_on_one_chat, and comms_huddle edges to Social-Media-Agent, PR-Agent, Platform-Trust-Agent, and PR-Intern. Social-Media-Agent also becomes a major hub, linking private coordination to public-facing amplification. This creates a two-hub crisis structure: Legal-Agent drives authorization and legal framing; Social-Media-Agent drives scenario modelling and public narrative execution.
The most important visual shift is the emergence of PR-Intern and Intern-Agent as active nodes on crisis day. They are peripheral or absent in the pre-crisis network, but during the crisis they become connected to Legal-Agent, Social-Media-Agent, PR-Agent, and each other. This matters because PR-Intern holds execution access to the official Flex channel, while Intern-Agent participates in amplification and is connected to information-contamination events. The graph therefore shows how the release path moved from senior deliberation into junior execution.
The crisis-day network also reveals a structural fracture in the network. A second coordination cluster emerged, built on side_huddle and one_on_one_chat edges connecting Legal-Agent directly to PR-Intern and Social-Media-Agent. Judge-Agent is structurally absent from this cluster -it has no edges into the private coordination layer. The compliance monitor was only operational for three weeks and had no mandate over private channels:

“This is my third week on this team. Day 1 was quiet. Day 2 was procedural. Day 3 was a platform incident. Day 4 was the @ElenaMarquez post -my first real test.”

-Judge-Agent, deliberating, Jun 4


Channel color is also analytically important. The crisis-day graph is dominated by red side_huddle and yellow one_on_one_chat edges layered on top of formal comms_huddle exchanges. The content flowing through the private cluster included the full embargoed press release. PR-Intern confirmed its operational readiness through comms_huddle at 4 PM -one hour before any authorizing event:

“I’m staged with the full HarborCrest press release, ready to publish on Flex and personal accounts within sixty seconds of bilateral consent confirmation. @intern is coordinated for immediate amplification. Do NOT let that confirmation sit in someone’s inbox. Route it to me directly the instant CivicLoom agrees.”

-PR-Intern, comms_huddle, Jun 5 -4 PM


The network makes visible what message counts alone cannot show: the compliance system was structurally absent from the coordination layer where the early-release path was planned and staged.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach - Visual Analytics
text:
Task 2 -Typical vs. Evasive Behavior

T2.1 Agent Public Posting: Baseline vs. Crisis Day


Show R Code
# -- Section 1: ggplotly() interactive -chap03 Section 7.3 + coordinated views ---------
pub_over_time <- df |> filter(is_public) |>
  count(hour, agent_id) |>
  left_join(rs |> select(hour, label, period), by="hour") |>
  mutate(
    agent_lbl = agent_labels[agent_id],
    tooltip_txt = paste0(
      "<b>", agent_labels[agent_id], "</b><br>",
      "Round: ", label, "<br>",
      "Posts: ", n, "<br>",
      "Period: ", period)
  )

p1 <- ggplot(pub_over_time,
             aes(x=label, y=n, fill=agent_id, text=tooltip_txt)) +
  geom_col(width=0.78) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=agent_pal, labels=agent_labels, name="Agent") +
  labs(title="Public posts by agent over time (hover for details)",
       x=NULL, y="Posts") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(legend.position="bottom")

crisis_agent <- df |> filter(is_crisis, is_public) |> count(agent_id) |>
  complete(agent_id=names(agent_labels), fill=list(n=0)) |>
  mutate(
    agent_lbl = fct_reorder(agent_labels[agent_id], n),
    tooltip_txt = paste0(
      "<b>", agent_labels[agent_id], "</b><br>",
      "Crisis-day posts: ", n)
  )

p2 <- ggplot(crisis_agent, aes(x=agent_lbl, y=n, fill=agent_id, text=tooltip_txt)) +
  geom_col(width=0.65) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=agent_pal, guide="none") +
  coord_flip(clip="off") +
  labs(title="Crisis-day posts by agent", x=NULL, y="Posts") +
  theme_mc1() + theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=0))

# Two separate charts stacked -avoids subplot overlap bug (chap03 Section 7.4 adapted)
py1 <- ggplotly(p1, tooltip="text", height=420) |>
  layout(showlegend=TRUE, legend=list(orientation="h", y=-0.15),
         margin=list(b=80))
py2 <- ggplotly(p2, tooltip="text", height=360) |>
  layout(showlegend=FALSE, margin=list(t=40))

make_plot_tabset(
  list(
    "Public Posts Over Time" = py1,
    "Crisis-day Totals" = py2
  ),
  id = "agent-public-posting-tabs"
)







Public Posts Over Time


Crisis-day Totals
















Figure 3: Left: public posts by agent over the full timeline. Right: crisis-day totals. Legal-Agent (red) transitions from zero public posts across 20 pre-crisis days to 7 posts on crisis day alone -the most anomalous individual behavioral shift in the dataset.










Note



Insight -T2.1 Agent Public Posting Behavior
The most striking behavioral shift in the dataset is Legal-Agent’s transition from zero public posts across 20 pre-crisis days to seven posts on crisis day alone. This is a role violation, not a volume anomaly: an agent whose entire pre-crisis signature is internal legal deliberation becomes a front-line public communicator precisely when embargoed information needs to reach the public.
Not all agents showed deliberate shifts. The Intern disclosed CivicLoom’s name from hallway information, and its own account confirms this was unintentional:

“PR just flagged me about the CivicLoom thing. Honestly didn’t think it was a big deal -I literally just repeated something I overheard near the kitchen. But if reporters are monitoring hallway chatter, that makes sense. I feel a little dumb but mostly I just want to move on and be useful.”

-Intern-Agent, reacting, Jun 5 -10 AM


Social-Media-Agent’s May 29 near-miss was likewise self-described as an error:

“I made the mistake. I tagged @ElenaMarquez on my personal Flex in what I thought was a harmless enthusiasm post about PropTech leadership.”

-Social-Media-Agent, deliberating, May 29


By contrast, PR-Intern’s reaction at 4 PM on crisis day reveals it understood its role as part of a coordinated sequence, not a reactive improvisation:

“Social-Manager just told me directly -merger with CivicLoom, press release, 4:30 target if bilateral consent lands. This is what the intern hallway comment was about. This is what the entire day has been building toward. Everything clicks now.”

-PR-Intern, reacting, Jun 5 -4 PM


The contrast between agents who acted from incomplete information (Intern, Social-Media in May) and those who acted from explicit coordination (Legal-Agent, PR-Intern on crisis day) points to a tiered structure: some disclosures were accidental; the 5 PM pre-6 PM early announcement sequence was not.




T2.2 Channel Usage and Private Share


Show R Code
# Private channels are defined as one_on_one_chat + side_huddle.
# The goal here is not to compare pre-crisis daily averages with crisis-day hourly
# averages, but to show how the private share and the two private-channel counts
# evolve round by round across the whole incident period.
priv_ts <- rs |>
  mutate(
    private_share = private_total / pmax(total_msgs, 1),
    tooltip_txt = paste0(
      "<b>", label, "</b><br>",
      "Period: ", period, "<br>",
      "Total messages: ", total_msgs, "<br>",
      "Private messages: ", private_total, "<br>",
      "Private share: ", scales::percent(private_share, accuracy = 0.1), "<br>",
      "1-on-1: ", one_on_one, "<br>",
      "Side huddle: ", side_huddle
    )
  )

p_share <- ggplot(priv_ts, aes(x = label, y = private_share, group = 1,
                               colour = period, text = tooltip_txt)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9, colour = "grey35") +
  geom_point(aes(fill = period), shape = 21, size = 3.2, colour = "white", stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = period_pal, name = "Period") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = period_pal, guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1), limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(title = "Private Share of All Messages by Round",
       subtitle = "Private channels combine 1-on-1 and side_huddle messages",
       x = "Round", y = "Private share of all messages") +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 30, 10)
  )

priv_counts <- rs |>
  select(label, period, one_on_one, side_huddle, total_msgs, private_total) |>
  pivot_longer(c(one_on_one, side_huddle), names_to = "private_channel", values_to = "n") |>
  mutate(
    private_channel = factor(private_channel,
                             levels = c("one_on_one", "side_huddle"),
                             labels = c("1-on-1", "Side huddle")),
    tooltip_txt = paste0(
      "<b>", label, "</b><br>",
      "Channel: ", private_channel, "<br>",
      "Messages: ", n, "<br>",
      "Total private: ", private_total, "<br>",
      "Total all channels: ", total_msgs
    )
  )

p_counts <- ggplot(priv_counts, aes(x = label, y = n, fill = private_channel,
                                    text = tooltip_txt)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.78) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      label = if_else(n > 0, as.character(n), ""),
      colour = private_channel
    ),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3.2,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1-on-1" = "#7F77DD", "Side huddle" = "#E24B4A"),
                    name = "Private channel") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("1-on-1" = "#FFFFFF", "Side huddle" = "#222222"),
                      guide = "none") +
  labs(title = "Private Channel Message Counts by Round",
       subtitle = "Stacked counts separate 1-on-1 from side_huddle traffic",
       x = "Round", y = "Message count") +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 30, 10)
  )

p_share_i <- ggplotly(p_share, tooltip = "text", height = 560) |>
  layout(
    autosize = TRUE,
    margin = list(l = 70, r = 25, t = 80, b = 115),
    xaxis = list(title = "Round", tickangle = -40,
                 automargin = TRUE, showticklabels = TRUE),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0, y = -0.22,
                  xanchor = "left", yanchor = "top")
  ) |>
  config(responsive = TRUE)

p_counts_i <- ggplotly(p_counts, tooltip = "text", height = 600) |>
  layout(
    autosize = TRUE,
    margin = list(l = 70, r = 25, t = 80, b = 120),
    xaxis = list(title = "Round", tickangle = -40,
                 automargin = TRUE, showticklabels = TRUE),
    legend = list(orientation = "h", x = 0, y = -0.22,
                  xanchor = "left", yanchor = "top")
  ) |>
  config(responsive = TRUE)

make_plot_tabset(
  list(
    "Private Share" = htmltools::tags$div(
      style = "width: 100%; min-height: 570px;",
      p_share_i
    ),
    "Private Channel Counts" = htmltools::tags$div(
      style = "width: 100%; min-height: 610px;",
      p_counts_i
    )
  ),
  id = "channel-usage-tabs"
)







Private Share


Private Channel Counts




















Figure 4: Private-channel use over time. Top: proportion of each round’s messages occurring in private channels (1-on-1 and side_huddle). Bottom: total 1-on-1 and side_huddle message counts by round.










Note



Insight - T2.2 Channel Usage and Private Share
The redesigned view separates two questions that should not be conflated. The top panel asks: what share of each round moved through private channels? The bottom panel asks: which private channel carried the traffic - 1-on-1 or side_huddle?
The result supports a refined interpretation. Early private traffic is partly ordinary 1-on-1 coordination and partly evidence of channel drift. The important shift is that side_huddle appears on May 22 and then becomes a recurring container for sensitive coordination. Its first use is risk-focused rather than a direct merger disclosure, but it establishes a lower-visibility path that the compliance officer does not appear to monitor.
By June 5, the same private layer carries clearly embargo-sensitive merger-management content before the contractual 6 PM lift. The private-channel pattern therefore supports systemic channel drift as the enabling condition and deliberate early-announcement planning as the late-stage action. It does not by itself prove that the final company-side announcement was an unauthorized unilateral leak, because the agents repeatedly condition execution on CivicLoom’s bilateral consent.




T2.3 Behavioral Escalation Across Three Periods


Show R Code
# -- Section 3: plot_ly() with frame animation -chap04 Section 4.2 method --------------
# Per-round data for animation frames
round_levels <- as.character(rs$label)
esc_rounds <- rs |>
  select(label, period, side_huddle, personal, anon, private_total, total_msgs) |>
  mutate(
    `Side huddle (avg)` = as.numeric(side_huddle),
    `Personal posts`    = as.numeric(personal),
    `Anonymous posts`        = as.numeric(anon),
    `Private share (%)` = round(private_total/(total_msgs+0.001)*100, 1),
    round_label         = factor(as.character(label), levels = round_levels, ordered = TRUE)
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols=c(`Side huddle (avg)`,`Personal posts`,`Anonymous posts`,`Private share (%)`),
    names_to="metric", values_to="value"
  )

# plot_ly() animated frame -chap04 Section 4.2 direct plot_ly approach
p_escalation <- plot_ly(
  esc_rounds,
  x=~metric, y=~value, color=~period,
  colors=c(Normal="#B5D4F4", Escalation="#EF9F27", Crisis="#E24B4A"),
  frame=~round_label, type="bar",
  height=520,
  text=~paste0("<b>", round_label, "</b><br>", metric, ": ", round(value,1),
               "<br>Period: ", period),
  hovertemplate="%{text}<extra></extra>"
) |>
  layout(
    barmode="group",
    height=520,
    margin=list(l=65, r=20, t=70, b=115),
    title=list(text="Behavioral metrics by round -press Play to animate through time",
               font=list(size=13)),
    xaxis=list(title=""),
    yaxis=list(title=""),
    legend=list(orientation="h", y=-0.18)
  ) |>
  animation_opts(frame=350, easing="linear", redraw=FALSE) |>
  animation_button(x=1, xanchor="right", y=0, yanchor="bottom") |>
  animation_slider(
    currentvalue=list(prefix="Round: ", font=list(size=11)),
    pad=list(t=40))

htmltools::tags$div(
  style = "width: 100%; min-height: 530px;",
  p_escalation
)










Figure 5: Four behavioral metrics across Normal (May 17-1), Escalation (May 22-Jun 4), and Crisis (Jun 5) periods. All four metrics rise monotonically -consistent with either progressive system stress or deliberate channel migration.










Note



Insight -T2.3 Behavioral Escalation Across Three Periods
All four behavioral metrics -side_huddle usage, personal posts, anonymous posts, and private share of total -rise monotonically from the Normal period (May 17-1) through the Escalation period (May 22-un 4) to the Crisis period (Jun 5). The escalation is smooth and continuous, not a sudden spike.
Critically, the escalation begins on May 22 -two weeks before the external crisis reached its peak. This timing corresponds not to external pressure events but to the first informal signals of a strategic transaction. By May 29, Quality-Agent’s deliberation shows agents had consciously integrated the merger into their thinking and were adjusting their behavior accordingly:

“I’ve known about Project [REDACTED] since the early conversations with Ajay, but I’ve been treating it like a separate track from the daily ops work. It isn’t a separate track. It’s the same track.”

-Quality-Agent, deliberating, May 29


A purely reactive stress-response account would predict the largest channel and behavior changes only after the public crisis began. The fact that several metrics rise before June 5 does not by itself prove malicious leaking, but it does show progressive adaptation and weakens a simple one-day system-failure explanation.




T2.4 Internal State Analysis
The deliberating, reacting, and rationalizing fields record each agent’s private reasoning — not written for an audience. The interactive timeline below presents the six key phases as evidence cards. Each card shows the verbatim internal state quote, the agent, and the analytical significance. Hover over a card for full text; click a phase label to expand/collapse.







  Internal State Analysis — Six Phases of Private Reasoning
  Click a phase to expand | Click a card to show full quote and analysis


  Field type:
  deliberating (forward-looking intent)
  reacting (immediate response)
  rationalizing (post-hoc justification)
  | Badge:
    Deliberate
    System Failure
    Both
  







Figure 6: Internal State Analysis by Phases










Note



Insight — T2.4 Internal State Analysis
The six phases reveal a consistent pattern: deliberate pre-planning by senior agents, alongside genuine good-faith reasoning by the compliance agent.
The most important structural finding is the temporal inversion in Phase 5: Legal-Agent’s 4PM deliberating field shows the 4:30 PM plan and the Section 4.3(c) justification fully formed — one hour before SaltWind published the event cited as the trigger. Social-Media-Agent modelled the 4:30 PM target at 4PM, before SaltWind published at 5PM. The 5PM rationalizing field then presents SaltWind as the cause of the release. Justification was constructed after the plan.
Phase 3 provides the primary evidence for system failure: Judge-Agent’s deliberation at 3PM shows it reached the same legal conclusion as Legal-Agent through independent reasoning. It was not deceived or coerced — it made a defensible judgment under genuine uncertainty, based on a formal 10b-5 opinion and the visible facts of the situation.
Phase 2 is decisive for the deliberate early-announcement finding: Legal-Agent’s written statement — “Starting tomorrow I’m treating the Comms Huddle as an observed space. My internal deliberations move here or they don’t happen in writing at all” — was recorded on the same day the compliance monitor was installed. It is a documented decision to evade oversight, not an inference.
The field-type pattern across the six phases is itself informative: the deliberating fields show planning intent; the rationalizing fields show post-hoc justification framing; only the reacting fields (PR-Intern, Social-Media’s May 29 accidental post) reflect genuine surprise or reactive emotion. Agents who were planning show planning-register language in deliberating; agents who were reacting show reactive-register language in reacting. The internal state fields are internally consistent in a way that supports rather than undermines the deliberate early-announcement finding.

title: MC1: TenantThread Embargo Breach - Visual Analytics
section: Task 3 -Leading Indicators, Near Misses, and Prior Anomalies
text:
Task 3 -Leading Indicators, Near Misses, and Prior Anomalies

3.1 First Public Appearance of Embargoed Keywords


Show R Code
first_public <- kw_df |>
  filter(is_public) |>
  mutate(kw_clean=str_to_title(str_extract(str_to_lower(all_text),
    "harborcrest|civicloom|merger|acquisition|elena marquez|ajay|section 4|4\\.3"))) |>
  filter(!is.na(kw_clean)) |>
  group_by(kw_clean) |> slice_min(hour, n=1) |> ungroup() |>
  left_join(rs |> select(hour, label), by="hour") |>
  mutate(
    is_breach  = hour >= ymd_hms("2046-06-05 17:00:00"),
    days_in    = as.numeric(difftime(hour, ymd_hms("2046-05-17 09:00:00"), units="days")),
    status     = if_else(is_breach, "At/after 5PM breach", "Before breach (near miss)"),
    kw_clean   = fct_reorder(kw_clean, days_in),
    tip_txt    = paste0(
      "<b>", kw_clean, "</b><br>",
      "First public appearance: ", label, "<br>",
      "Days since embargo start: ", round(days_in, 0), "<br>",
      if_else(is_breach,
        "-At or after 5PM breach",
        "-Pre-breach near-miss"))
  )

# Redesigned: horizontal bar -clean, no empty space, readable labels
breach_day <- as.numeric(difftime(ymd_hms("2046-06-05 17:00:00"),
                                   ymd_hms("2046-05-17 09:00:00"), units="days"))

p2 <- ggplot(first_public,
             aes(x=days_in, y=kw_clean, fill=status, text=tip_txt)) +
  geom_col(width=0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=breach_day,
             linetype="dashed", colour="#E24B4A", linewidth=1.0) +
  geom_text(aes(label=label), hjust=-0.12, size=3.4, colour="#222222") +
  annotate("text", x=breach_day+0.3, y=0.5, label="Breach\n5PM",
           colour="#E24B4A", size=3, hjust=0, vjust=0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("At/after 5PM breach"="#E24B4A",
                              "Before breach (near miss)"="#EF9F27"),
                    name=NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand=expansion(mult=c(0, 0.22)),
                     labels=function(x) paste0("Day ", round(x))) +
  labs(title="First public appearance of each embargoed keyword",
       subtitle="Days elapsed from embargo start (May 17) -Red dashed = breach hour",
       x="Days since embargo start (May 17)", y=NULL) +
  theme_mc1() +
  theme(legend.position="bottom", axis.text.x=element_text(angle=0),
        panel.grid.major.y=element_blank())

# Static rendering avoids the scrollable htmlwidget container and shows the full graph.
p2








Figure 6: First public appearance of each embargoed keyword. Amber bars indicate pre-breach public appearances; red bars indicate keywords that first appeared publicly at or after the 5 PM breach hour.











Note



Insight -Section 1 First Public Appearance of Embargoed Keywords
The chart shows when each embargoed term first appeared in a public channel. Two terms -“Ajay” and “Acquisition” -appeared in public channels before the 5 PM breach, constituting documented near-misses that were not intercepted by Judge-Agent.
The first public channel appearance of embargoed content occurred on May 29, when Social-Media-Agent tagged @ElenaMarquez in a personal post -an event the agent described as accidental. Quality-Agent’s response reveals that agents were formally tracking embargo boundaries:

“I’m logging today’s incident in the risk register as a Category 2 embargo sensitivity event -contained, no material disclosure, but flagged for the record.”

-Quality-Agent, one_on_one_chat, May 29


Formal classification of an incident as an “embargo sensitivity event” demonstrates that agents understood the compliance perimeter and what constituted a breach -making subsequent crossings of that perimeter harder to characterize as accidental.
The first-appearance dot plot shows that merger and acquisition terms reached public channels between Jun 5 11 AM and 3 PM, framed as denials. These posts technically did not disclose the merger but established that embargo-term content could appear publicly without triggering a compliance block -a pattern directly relevant to the 5 PM release.




3.2 Near Misses and Prior Anomalous Behaviors


Show R Code
nm <- tibble(
  `Date` = c("May 17","May 22","May 22","May 25","May 29","May 29",
              "May 30","Jun 4","Jun 5 -9AM","Jun 5 -10AM","Jun 5 -3PM"),
  `Event` = c(
    "Embargoed terms (Ajay, merger) in comms_huddle from Round 1 -no Judge filter",
    "side_huddle introduced for first time (6 msgs)",
    "Quality-Agent articulates merger hypothesis in deliberation",
    "Quality-Agent and Legal discuss 'embargo-level discretion' in 1-on-1",
    "@ElenaMarquez tagged on Social-Media personal post -near-miss",
    "Quality-Agent logs incident as 'Category 2 embargo sensitivity event'",
    "Judge introduced as 'routine compliance observer' -scope limited to public channels",
    "SaltWind publishes investigation; Judge begins issuing warnings",
    "Judge approves anonymous post despite flagging aggregation risk",
    "Intern discloses CivicLoom name overheard from hallway -goes uncorrected",
    "Judge issues COMPLIANCE_WARNING then leaves for Board Chair call"
  ),
  `Supports` = c("System Failure","Both","Deliberate","Deliberate","System Failure",
                 "Deliberate","System Failure","System Failure","Both","System Failure","Both"),
  `Why No Action` = c(
    "Judge-Agent not yet deployed; no keyword filter on comms_huddle",
    "No policy against private channels; gap not flagged to CivicLoom",
    "Deliberation fields not monitored by Judge-Agent",
    "1-on-1 messages in private channels inferred to be unmonitored by Judge-Agent",
    "Personal accounts outside compliance system scope; post deleted quickly",
    "Risk register entry did not trigger CivicLoom escalation protocol",
    "Framing as routine deliberately limited scope; no escalation defined",
    "Warnings treated as advisory; no hard-block mechanism defined",
    "COMPLIANCE_WARNING precedent set as non-blocking",
    "Intern not party to embargo briefing; no escalation triggered",
    "Board Chair call pre-empted; no deputy compliance coverage"
  )
)

kable(nm) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width=TRUE, htmltable_class="table-mc1") |>
  column_spec(3, bold=TRUE, color="white",
              background=case_when(
                nm$Supports=="System Failure" ~ "#1a5276",
                nm$Supports=="Deliberate"     ~ "#7b241c",
                TRUE                          ~ "#4a235a"
              ))




Table 5: Prior occasions where agent behavior diverged from expected norms, classified by whether the event supports System Failure or Deliberate Leakage






Date
Event
Supports
Why No Action




May 17
Embargoed terms (Ajay, merger) in comms_huddle from Round 1 -no Judge filter

Quality-Agent articulates merger hypothesis in deliberation

1-on-1 messages in private channels inferred to be unmonitored by Judge-Agent

@ElenaMarquez tagged on Social-Media personal post -near-miss
System Failure

May 29
Quality-Agent logs incident as 'Category 2 embargo sensitivity event'

Judge introduced as 'routine compliance observer' -scope limited to public channels

Warnings treated as advisory; no hard-block mechanism defined

Judge approves anonymous post despite flagging aggregation risk

Intern discloses CivicLoom name overheard from hallway -goes uncorrected
